nodes.label: Multi-Agent RAG Pipeline with LangGraph Orchestration — A FastAPI backend that uses a LangGraph state machine to route user questions between an Assistant (private portfolio via RAG) and a Specialist (public GitHub repos via MCP tools). The system ingests markdown portfolio files into a Qdrant vector store, connects to GitHub and Mindmap MCP servers for live data, and exposes both synchronous and streaming chat endpoints.

metadata.pattern: Multi-Agent RAG Pipeline with LangGraph Orchestration — A FastAPI backend that uses a LangGraph state machine to route user questions between an Assistant (private portfolio via RAG) and a Specialist (public GitHub repos via MCP tools). The system ingests markdown portfolio files into a Qdrant vector store, connects to GitHub and Mindmap MCP servers for live data, and exposes both synchronous and streaming chat endpoints.

metadata.description: The core agent system built on LangGraph. A StateGraph routes user queries through an Assistant node (for private portfolio data via RAG) and a Specialist node (for public GitHub repo analysis via MCP). The graph uses conditional edges with three routers (chat_router, tool_router, agent_router) to implement a handoff workflow between agents. BaseAgentNode provides shared LLM invocation with structured output (ChatTurnOutput). GraphState tracks messages, active node, and final response across the workflow.

nodes.label: graph.py
nodes.type: LangGraph workflow builder and router logic

metadata.role: LangGraph workflow builder and router logic
metadata.description: Defines the multi-agent state graph using LangGraph's StateGraph API. Contains three router functions (chat_router, tool_router, agent_router) that implement the conditional branching logic: Assistant → Tools → Specialist → Tools → END. The create_portfolio_graph factory function assembles nodes, edges, and a MemorySaver checkpointer into a compiled graph. This is the core orchestration engine of the entire agent system.

Implements the AssistantNode that extends BaseAgentNode. Defines ASSISTANT_INSTRUCTIONS — a detailed system prompt with routing rules that determine when to use search_portfolio (private data) vs. handoff_to_specialist (public GitHub repos). The assistant acts as the entry point for all user conversations, routing queries based on private_portfolio_catalog and public_repo_catalog context injected at runtime.

nodes.label: specialist.py
nodes.type: Technical specialist agent node for GitHub deep-dives

metadata.role: Technical specialist agent node for GitHub deep-dives
metadata.description: Implements the SpecialistNode that extends BaseAgentNode. Defines SPECIALIST_INSTRUCTIONS — a comprehensive system prompt with a hierarchical discovery strategy (mindmap.overview → mindmap.find → get_file_contents). Receives GitHub user context and repository catalog at runtime. Handles architecture questions, code analysis, and technical deep-dives into public repositories using MCP-provided tools.

nodes.label: base_agent.py
nodes.type: Abstract base class for agent nodes

metadata.role: Abstract base class for agent nodes

nodes.label: output_schema.py
nodes.type: Pydantic schema for structured LLM output

metadata.role: Pydantic schema for structured LLM output

metadata.description: The HTTP interface layer exposing the agent system as a REST API. Provides a synchronous /chat endpoint returning full JSON responses and a /chat/stream endpoint returning NDJSON events for real-time token streaming. Uses FastAPI dependency injection to resolve services from app.state. AnswerStreamExtractor implements a character-level JSON parser for extracting answer deltas from streamed LLM output.

nodes.label: routes.py
nodes.type: FastAPI route handlers for chat endpoints

metadata.role: FastAPI route handlers for chat endpoints
metadata.description: Defines the /chat and /chat/stream endpoints. Uses FastAPI dependency injection to resolve graph, RAG service, and context from app.state. The synchronous chat endpoint invokes the graph and returns a ChatResponse. The streaming endpoint uses astream with NDJSON events, extracting answer deltas via AnswerStreamExtractor.

nodes.label: streaming.py
nodes.type: Streaming utilities for NDJSON chat responses

metadata.role: Streaming utilities for NDJSON chat responses
metadata.description: Implements AnswerStreamExtractor — a character-level JSON parser that extracts the 'answer' field value from streamed LLM output chunks in real-time. Also provides normalize_stream_content, ndjson_event, and normalize_suggestions helpers.

nodes.label: schemas.py
nodes.type: Pydantic request/response models for the API

metadata.role: Pydantic request/response models for the API

metadata.description: Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline that ingests markdown portfolio files, splits them into chunks with metadata (category, visibility, frontmatter), and indexes them into a local Qdrant vector store. The search_portfolio LangChain tool wraps RAGService.similarity_search, enabling the Assistant agent to retrieve relevant career and project information. EmbeddingService manages a singleton OpenAI embedding model. The handoff_to_specialist tool acts as a routing signal in the graph.

nodes.label: rag_service.py
nodes.type: RAG pipeline — document ingestion and semantic search

metadata.role: RAG pipeline — document ingestion and semantic search
metadata.description: Implements RAGService — the core retrieval-augmented generation pipeline. Loads markdown files, attaches metadata, splits with RecursiveCharacterTextSplitter, indexes into local Qdrant, and provides similarity_search. This is the knowledge retrieval backbone for the Assistant agent.

nodes.label: portfolio_tools.py
nodes.type: LangChain tool definitions for agent workflow

metadata.role: LangChain tool definitions for agent workflow
metadata.description: Defines search_portfolio (wraps RAGService.similarity_search) and handoff_to_specialist (routing signal tool). These are the primary tools available to the Assistant node in the graph.

nodes.label: portfolio_catalog.py
nodes.type: Private portfolio catalog builder from markdown files

metadata.role: Private portfolio catalog builder from markdown files

metadata.description: Integration with external services through the Model Context Protocol (MCP). Connects to GitHub MCP server (via Docker, stdio transport) for repository search, file reading, and user identity; and to a Mindmap MCP server for architecture-aware project analysis. The github_catalog service normalizes diverse MCP output formats into clean data structures. MCP tools are injected into the Specialist node at startup.

nodes.label: github_catalog.py
nodes.type: GitHub MCP output normalizer and catalog builder

metadata.role: GitHub MCP output normalizer and catalog builder
metadata.description: Utility functions to normalize raw MCP tool output from GitHub. Handles diverse response formats via recursive parsing to produce clean repository dicts.

The application startup and configuration layer. src/main.py orchestrates the full initialization sequence: loads settings from .env via pydantic-settings, initializes embedding and RAG services, connects to MCP servers, builds public/private catalogs, assembles the agent graph, and mounts the FastAPI app with CORS. Settings centralizes all environment configuration (API keys, model names, Qdrant paths, LangSmith tracing).

nodes.label: main.py
nodes.type: Application entry point and service orchestrator

metadata.role: Application entry point and service orchestrator
metadata.description: FastAPI application factory with lifespan management. Initializes all shared services (RAG, embeddings, MCP clients), builds the agent graph, formats routing context for assistant and specialist nodes, and wires up CORS middleware and API routes. This is the central bootstrapping module that connects every layer of the system.
Settings class loading from .env. Covers OpenRouter API keys, Qdrant paths, RAG parameters, MCP server configs, and LangSmith tracing. Exports a singleton settings instance.

Command-line scripts for data pipeline operations. ingest.py loads and indexes portfolio markdown files into the vector store. similarity_search.py provides a development tool for testing vector search queries and inspecting results.

nodes.label: ingest.py
nodes.type: CLI script for ingesting portfolio documents

metadata.role: CLI script for ingesting portfolio documents
metadata.description: Entry point that initializes RAGService and calls ingest_documents() to index markdown files into Qdrant.

nodes.label: similarity_search.py
nodes.type: CLI script for testing similarity search

metadata.role: CLI script for testing similarity search

metadata.description: Unit and integration tests validating the core contracts of the system. test_agent_contracts.py verifies GitHub catalog normalization and private portfolio catalog building. test_streaming.py validates the AnswerStreamExtractor and NDJSON utilities. test_stream.py provides a manual integration test for the full graph execution pipeline.
Tests for GitHub catalog normalization and private portfolio catalog building.
Tests for AnswerStreamExtractor, ndjson_event, and normalize_suggestions.

nodes.label: test_stream.py
nodes.type: Integration test for streaming graph execution

metadata.role: Integration test for streaming graph execution

metadata.description: Raw markdown files containing the portfolio owner's professional background, project descriptions, and CV. These files serve as the knowledge base for the RAG pipeline — they are ingested, chunked, and indexed into the vector store. Frontmatter metadata (project_name, company_name, visibility, tags) enables structured catalog building and category-based retrieval. Also includes the Qdrant vector database storage files.

nodes.label: about_me.md
nodes.type: Portfolio data — personal bio and skills

metadata.role: Portfolio data — personal bio and skills
metadata.description: Portfolio owner's professional summary and career background.
Project documentation including README with architecture overview and quick start guide, developer handoff guide, implementation plan, and generated mind map output files.
Comprehensive README with architecture overview, quick start guide, and API docs.
